title: Convert RaftMessage from flat optional interface to discriminated union
acceptanceCriteria: RaftMessage is a discriminated union with 4 variants
Each variant has only the fields for that message type (no optionals for wrong fields)
handleMessage switch cases narrow types correctly
All existing tests pass
matchIndex on AppendEntriesResponse replaces the `as` cast at line 739

title: Replace MRStep.data: Record<string, unknown> with discriminated union per phase
acceptanceCriteria: MRStep is a discriminated union with 5 variants
Zero `as` casts in MapReduceCanvas rendering code
TypeScript narrows data fields correctly in phase-based switch
simulateMapReduce return type is MRStep[]

title: Fix unsafe double-cast: events as unknown as RaftEvent[] at DistributedModule.tsx:4809
description:
The GenericEvent[] array (with 3 fields: tick, type, description) is cast to RaftEvent[] (which has 5 fields: tick, type, nodeId, description, data). The double-cast `as unknown as` bypasses type checking entirely. At runtime, nodeId and data are undefined.

However, RaftCanvas never reads the events prop — it uses nodes, leader, crashedNodes, pendingMessages instead. The events prop appears to be dead code.

Fix: Either (a) remove the events prop from RaftCanvas since it's not used, or (b) pass the actual RaftEvent[] from the engine instead of the flattened generic events.
acceptanceCriteria: No `as unknown as` cast remains
Either events prop removed from RaftCanvas or typed correctly
TypeScript compiles without any unsafe casts in this file

title: Unify step discriminator field name: type vs phase vs action
description:
Three different names for the event/step discriminator field across engines:
- `type`: RaftEvent, CAPEvent, CRDTEvent, ClockEvent, LamportEvent
- `phase`: TwoPCStep, MRStep, PaxosStep
- `action`: SagaStep

This prevents writing generic step-processing code (e.g., a shared event log renderer) because the discriminator field name varies. The GenericEvent type at :552 uses `type` — so events with `phase` or `action` must be manually mapped.

Standardize on `type` (the most common, matches TypeScript discriminated union convention). Rename TwoPCStep.phase→type, MRStep.phase→type, PaxosStep.phase→type, SagaStep.action→type. Keep the original field as an alias if needed for backwards compat.
acceptanceCriteria: All step/event types use `type` as the discriminator field
GenericEvent mapping simplified (no field renaming needed)
UI code updated to read `type` instead of `phase`/`action`
All tests updated

title: Replace CRDTSimulation.getValue() return type from unknown to typed per-CRDTType
acceptanceCriteria: getValue() returns a type narrower than unknown
getAllValues() returns Record<string, CRDTValue> instead of Record<string, unknown>
CRDTEvent.resultValue typed as CRDTValue instead of unknown
formatValue in CRDTCanvas can pattern-match without runtime type guessing

title: Rename GenericEvent to SimulationLogEntry and add optional source fields
acceptanceCriteria: GenericEvent renamed to SimulationLogEntry
Optional sourceNodeId populated where engines provide it
Event log can show source node without unsafe casts
Severity field enables log color-coding without type-string matching

title: Prefix HashNode/HashKey with ConsistentHash for naming consistency
description:
consistent-hash.ts exports HashNode and HashKey — too generic. Every other engine uses algorithm-prefixed names (RaftNode, GossipNode, CAPNode). Rename to ConsistentHashNode and ConsistentHashKey for consistency.

Also rename OperationResult (cap-theorem.ts:18) to CAPOperationResult, and MRStep (map-reduce.ts:14) to MapReduceStep.
acceptanceCriteria: HashNode → ConsistentHashNode
HashKey → ConsistentHashKey
OperationResult → CAPOperationResult
MRStep → MapReduceStep
All imports updated across codebase
index.ts barrel export updated

title: Share ClockEventType between vector-clock.ts and lamport-timestamps.ts
description:
vector-clock.ts:19 defines `ClockEventType = 'local' | 'send' | 'receive'`. lamport-timestamps.ts:19 defines the same union inline as `type: 'local' | 'send' | 'receive'` in LamportEvent. Both are identical 3-member unions representing the same concept.

Extract to a shared type and import from both files. This ensures if a new event type is added (e.g., 'barrier'), both engines are updated.
acceptanceCriteria: Single ClockEventType definition used by both engines
LamportEvent.type uses the shared type
ClockEvent.type uses the shared type
No inline redefinition of the same union

title: Define DistributedSimulation interface for compile-time exhaustiveness checking
acceptanceCriteria: DistributedSimulation interface defined
Registry object typed so missing sims are compile errors
Adding new sim to DistributedSim union forces implementing all parts
Existing if/else chain in canvas/controls/properties replaced with registry lookup

title: Add distributed module event emission to shared progress and cross-module stores
description:
The distributed module manages ALL state in local useState/useRef (45 variables in useDistributedModule hook). It calls ZERO shared stores — no progress-store.addXP(), no cross-module-store.updateModuleMastery(), no module-progress activity logging.

This is why the dashboard, gamification, and cross-module systems are disconnected.

Add store integration:
1. On simulation switch: log activity to module-progress
2. On completing a full simulation run: call progress-store.addXP()
3. On exploring a new feature: call markFeatureExplored in module-progress
4. On completing all sims: call cross-module-store.updateModuleMastery()
acceptanceCriteria: Simulation interactions appear in module-progress activity log
XP awarded for completing simulation runs
Feature exploration tracked (6/11 → 11/11 features)
Cross-module mastery updated when user explores distributed concepts
Dashboard shows correct distributed module progress
